title: ModelEngine-Group/nexent
description: Nexent is a zero-code platform for auto-generating production-grade AI agents using Harness Engineering principles — unified tools, skills, memory, and orchestration with built-in constraints, feedback loops, and control planes.

title: AltimateAI/altimate-code
description: Open-source agentic data engineering harness for dbt, SQL, and cloud warehouses. 100+ tools, 10 warehouses, AI-powered.

title: Picrew/awesome-agent-harness
description: An awesome list of Agent Harness engineering resources, including GitHub projects, tools, benchmarks, and practical guides.

title: affaan-m/everything-claude-code
description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.

title: bytedance/deer-flow
description: An open-source long-horizon SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skill, subagents and message gateway, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.

title: can1357/oh-my-pi
description: ⌥  AI Coding agent for the terminal — hash-anchored edits, optimized tool harness, LSP, Python, browser, subagents, and more

title: e10nMa2k/cc-mini
description: Ultra-light Harness scaffolding for AI agents, a mini version of claude code

title: agent-sh/agnix
description: The missing linter and lsp for AI coding assistants. Validate CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, hooks, MCP. Plugin for all major IDEs included, with autofixes.

A Model Context Protocol Server for Quantum Execution in Hybrid Quantum-HPC Environments
description: The integration of large language models (LLMs) into scientific research is accelerating the realization of autonomous ``AI Scientists.'' While recent advancements have empowered AI to formulate hypotheses and design experiments, a critical gap remains in the execution of these tasks, particularly in the domain of quantum computing (QC). Executing quantum algorithms requires not only generating co
authors: Masaki Shiraishi, Ikko Hamamura, Tatsuya Ishigaki

title: RemoteAgent: Bridging Vague Human Intents and Earth Observation with RL-based Agentic MLLMs
description: Earth Observation (EO) systems are essentially designed to support domain experts who often express their requirements through vague natural language rather than precise, machine-friendly instructions. Depending on the specific application scenario, these vague queries can demand vastly different levels of visual precision. Consequently, a practical EO AI system must bridge the gap between ambiguo
authors: Liang Yao, Shengxiang Xu, Fan Liu

title: Multi-Agent Orchestration for High-Throughput Materials Screening on a Leadership-Class System
description: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) with High-Performance Computing (HPC) is transforming scientific workflows from human-directed pipelines into adaptive systems capable of autonomous decision-making. Large language models (LLMs) play a critical role in autonomous workflows; however, deploying LLM-based agents at scale remains a significant challenge. Single-agent architectures and se
authors: Thang Duc Pham, Harikrishna Tummalapalli, Fakhrul Hasan Bhuiyan

title: MCP-DPT: A Defense-Placement Taxonomy and Coverage Analysis for Model Context Protocol Security
description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) enables large language models (LLMs) to dynamically discover and invoke third-party tools, significantly expanding agent capabilities while introducing a distinct security landscape. Unlike prompt-only interactions, MCP exposes pre-execution artifacts, shared context, multi-turn workflows, and third-party supply chains to adversarial influence across independently
authors: Mehrdad Rostamzadeh, Sidhant Narula, Nahom Birhan

title: Agent-Driven Corpus Linguistics: A Framework for Autonomous Linguistic Discovery
description: Corpus linguistics has traditionally relied on human researchers to formulate hypotheses, construct queries, and interpret results - a process demanding specialized technical skills and considerable time. We propose Agent-Driven Corpus Linguistics, an approach in which a large language model (LLM), connected to a corpus query engine via a structured tool-use interface, takes over the investigative
authors: Jia Yu, Weiwei Yu, Pengfei Xiao
